task_completion.description: Create at least 3 partition walls for enclosed spaces and add at least 3 doors.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 2D plan view, look for newly drawn thick lines representing interior walls that divide the open space into separate rooms. Look for at least 3 door icons/models placed within these or other walls.

task_completion.description: Define at least 4 room zones with descriptive names.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 2D plan view, look for shaded floor areas with text labels indicating distinct zones (e.g., 'Retail Showroom', 'Cafe', 'Workshop', 'Office', 'Restroom'). There must be at least 4 distinct labeled rooms.

task_completion.description: Furnish the Workshop with >=2 workbenches and >=3 storage units, the Restroom with >=1 toilet, and include >=1 sink globally.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 2D or 3D view, locate the Workshop area and verify it contains at least 2 workbench/table items and 3 storage units (cabinets/shelves). Locate the Restroom area and verify it has a toilet. Verify there is at least 1 sink visible anywhere in the design.

task_completion.description: Furnish the Cafe with >=1 service counter, >=4 seating items, and >=1 kitchen appliance, and the Retail Showroom with >=4 display units.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 2D or 3D view, locate the Cafe area and verify it has a counter, at least 4 chairs/stools, and an appliance (e.g., espresso machine, fridge). Locate the Retail Showroom and verify it has at least 4 display units (racks, shelves, or display cases).

task_completion.description: Include at least 2 dimension lines and save the file as 'bicycle_shop_result.sh3d'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 2D plan view, look for at least 2 dimension lines (lines with measurement numbers indicating clearances or lengths). Check the Sweet Home 3D window title bar or the save dialog for the filename 'bicycle_shop_result.sh3d'.

integrity.description: The agent must use the Sweet Home 3D graphical interface to complete the design.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Sweet Home 3D application open with the agent actively using the mouse/tools to draw walls, create rooms, and place furniture in the 2D/3D panes.

integrity.description: The agent must build the design incrementally rather than loading a completed file.
integrity.visual_evidence: A sequence of screenshots should show the design evolving from an empty open-plan shell to a fully furnished space, confirming the agent did not just open a pre-existing solution.

integrity.description: The agent must not bypass the GUI by editing the .sh3d file directly via text or hex editors.
integrity.visual_evidence: No text editors or terminal windows should be shown modifying the raw XML/JSON or binary contents of the .sh3d file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected result file must be saved as 'bicycle_shop_result.sh3d' in ~/Documents/SweetHome3D/. The pass threshold is 70/100 points. Note a logical flaw in the programmatic verifier: although the task requires two sinks (one in the workshop, one in the restroom), the verifier only checks for a single global sink (sink_count >= 1). Therefore, award full points for the sink requirement if at least one sink is visible anywhere in the design.
